_meta.purpose: Decision lattice with pruning for TRAUMA_ACTIVATION_CRITERIA. Replaces the flat string arrays in accreditation_measures.json -> criteria with walkable, prunable data. The walker is code; every mechanism, criterion and subcategory is a ROW. New mechanisms = new rows, not new code.
_meta.status: PROPOSAL — pending clinician sign-off (Damian Mosher, ER MD) + coder review. NOT signed off. See ../CLINICIAN_SIGNOFF_ADDENDUM.md.
_meta.replaces: accreditation_measures.json -> TRAUMA_ACTIVATION_CRITERIA.criteria (the three flat string arrays)
_meta.scope_note: DATA IS DELIBERATELY NARROW, and the cutoff is CUMULATIVE CASE COVERAGE — not phrase frequency, not importance. Pick the mechanism families that cover ~78% of cases; their verbiage comes along for the ride. Skip crocodile bites -> skip crocodile-bite vocabulary. The case list IS the vocabulary list.

coverage.claim: Falls, motor vehicle traffic, firearm, and cut/pierce account for ~78% of US trauma registry cases.
coverage.source: ACS National Trauma Data Bank Annual Report 2016 (admission year 2015; N=861,888; 747 facilities). Last edition — ACS retired the annual reports in 2016; TQP successors are participant-gated. Cite bibliographically, NOT by hyperlink (ACS no longer hosts the PDF).
coverage.corroboration: ~78% independently in three cohorts: 78.49% (NTDB 2016) · 74.52% (Tomas, Inj Prev 2023, NTDB 2012-2019, N=5,630,461) · 78.3% (Alfieri, NTDB 2019-21, ISS>15). 4-point spread.

families_added_7_17._of: Firearm 4.21 + Cut/pierce 4.13 — two NTDB mechanisms, one ACS criterion

coverage.🔴_say_registry_cases_never_activations: STRUCTURAL, not a literature gap. NTDB 2016 contains ZERO instances of 'activation'. The NTDS element HIGHESTACTIVATION is binary, HOSPITAL-DEFINED, and only entered the research file in 2021 — a national mechanism-by-activation distribution is NOT COMPUTABLE. Single-center data proves why: blunt share among full activations runs 44.5% (Ben Taub) to 91.5% (Fakhry, 36 centers); full-activation RATE varies 3.3%-54.1% because each hospital sets its own top tier. If asked about activations, the honest answer is that the national data element does not support the question.
coverage.do_not_add_auto_vs_ped: Double-counts — MVT is an E-code umbrella already containing pedestrian. See MECH_AUTO_VS_PED.
coverage.directional_only: Restricting to ISS>15 (closer to an activated population) drops falls 44.18 -> 35.0 and roughly DOUBLES firearm 4.21 -> 9.2. Category definitions differ between sources, so never compare the numbers directly — but it is a real argument that MECH_PENETRATING matters MORE for the sick population we actually serve than the all-registry table suggests.

_meta.design_source: fixtures/RECORDING_PACKET.md BUILD NOTE (mechanism-scoped number matching) + BUILD_SPEC.md 'DEMO AUDIO PATH'

walker._comment: The whole algorithm, as a contract. ~40 lines to implement. The tap-tap-tap cascade is EMERGENT — it is this loop re-running after each answer, not a scripted sequence.
steps: 1. ENTER: a family is entered when its enter_when lexicon matches. Multiple families may be entered.
2. PRUNE resolved: drop any node whose affirm OR negate already matched in the transcript. (Heard = never ask.)
3. PRUNE excluded: drop every node in a family listed in an entered family's excludes_families.
4. PRUNE blocked: drop any node whose depends_on is not yet resolved.
5. PRUNE immaterial: drop any node where material() is false. See materiality below.
6. RANK: sort survivors by info_gain desc, then by id for determinism.
7. RENDER: exactly ONE node — the top. Never N prompts.
8. On answer (or any new transcript evidence): goto 1. If a node survives, the SAME prompt surface updates (the cascade). If none survives, the prompt clears itself.

materiality._comment: Two arms. An OR, not an AND. See OBLIGATIONS_MODEL.md section 3 — a qualification-only rule prunes the demo's opening beat, because SBP 88 is heard at [0:05] and the ejection prompt lands at [0:12].
materiality.safety: NOT SUPPORTED — deliberately. The tool prompts about the record, never about the care. See OBLIGATIONS_MODEL.md section 3.

walker.hard_invariant: A prompt is a pure render of state, never an event. Recompute from the FULL evidence set on every transcript update BEFORE consulting the renderer. Nothing schedules a prompt; clocks only mutate material().

families.label: Motor vehicle collision
families.exclusion_rationale: A distance heard under MVC is a THROW distance, never a fall height. MVC + ejected + any distance resolves to ejection; the distance is irrelevant. Never traverse MECH_FALL while MECH_MVC is entered. This is why BUILD_SPEC's corrected prompt is 'Was the patient ejected?' and NOT 'fall or ejection?' — and why SPLICE_NOTES.md:70 can trim 'that's not a fall' as answering a question nobody asks.

distance.note: Captured for the record; contributes to NO criterion under this family. This single field is the 'a bare number must never trigger a criterion' rule, expressed as data.

nodes.prompt: MVC confirmed. Heard "{distance}" — ejection not captured. Was the patient ejected?
nodes.prompt_fallback: MVC confirmed — ejection not captured. Was the patient ejected?
nodes.prompt_subline_documentation: Activation already qualifies ({qualifying_criterion}). Mechanism is required for the registry.
nodes.prompt_subline_qualification: Ejection would qualify the activation fee.
nodes._subline_note: Which subline renders depends on ADDENDUM Q1 (does a prehospital EMS-reported SBP satisfy the ED criterion?). The walker picks it from material() at render time — do not hard-code either one.

nodes.prompt: MVC, no ejection. High-risk crash features not captured — intrusion or prolonged extrication?
MVC — was there a death in the same compartment?
negate: no one else in the car

nodes.prompt: MVC — restraint status not captured. Was the patient restrained?
nodes._note: Qualifies NOTHING alone — restraint status is not an ACS criterion. It is registry-required, so it is documentation-material only. Included precisely to exercise the two-armed materiality rule: this node must NEVER produce a qualification prompt, and must be prunable independently of MVC_EJECTED. In the demo it is pruned at [0:00] ('unrestrained driver' is in the activation call).

enter_guard.note: MANDATORY. 'that's not a fall' appears TWICE per script (RECORDING_PACKET.md:111,113) and 'logging ejection, not a fall' once. Without negation-scoping, the demo's own disambiguation beat enters MECH_FALL and asks about fall height on an MVC patient — on stage, immediately after a human just said the opposite. See collision_corpus.jsonl class 'negation'.

distance.note: Same 'distance' modifier as MECH_MVC, opposite role. THIS ASYMMETRY IS THE ENTIRE RULE — 'fall'+>20ft qualifies, 'fall'+<=20ft explicitly does NOT, 'MVC'+any distance is irrelevant. Two data rows, no code.

nodes.prompt: Fall confirmed — height not captured. Over twenty feet?
nodes.prompt_with_orphan_distance: Fall confirmed. Heard "{distance}" — is that the fall height?
nodes._note: Resolved by the distance MODIFIER, not by a lexicon hit — this is the one node whose answer is a number. affirm/negate are empty ON PURPOSE. Evaluate: distance > 20 ft -> criterion met; distance <= 20 ft -> criterion NOT met (a short fall is a real, correct, NEGATIVE answer — it resolves the node to not_applicable, it does not leave it missing); no distance -> ambiguous -> prompt.

families.label: Penetrating trauma (firearm / cut-pierce)
families._coverage: Serves TWO NTDB registry mechanisms in ONE family: Firearm 4.21% + Cut/pierce 4.13% = 8.34% of US trauma registry cases (ACS NTDB Annual Report 2016, admission year 2015, N=861,888). Takes built coverage 70.15% -> 78.49%. See AGENT_LINEUP.md.
families._why_one_family: Because the registry taxonomy and the criterion taxonomy DO NOT ALIGN, and the model has to hold both. NTDB counts firearm and cut/pierce as separate mechanisms. The ACS activation criterion does not care about the weapon at all — it cares about penetration and SITE. So: `weapon` is a registry-only modifier (documentation-material), `site` is the qualifier (qualification-material). Same two-armed materiality as MVC_EJECTED. Splitting this into MECH_GSW and MECH_STAB would duplicate the site node to satisfy a distinction the criterion doesn't make.

enter_guard.note: Same guard as MECH_FALL. 'no penetrating injury' and 'he wasn't shot' are negations — a survey that rules penetrating trauma OUT must not enter the family.

families._no_exclusions: Deliberate. Penetrating and blunt genuinely co-occur (shot, then crashed the car). Unlike MVC/fall, these are not mutually exclusive — so no excludes_families. The data says so; the walker needs no special case.

weapon.note: Documentation-material, NEVER qualification-material. It is asked for the RECORD (NTDB mechanism coding), never for the money — the criterion is weapon-blind. Exactly the arm that saves the ejection prompt (OBLIGATIONS_MODEL.md section 3).

site.note: THE THIRD INSTANCE OF ONE RULE SHAPE, and the proof the model generalizes: `distance` under FALL = NUMERIC qualifier (>20 ft). `distance` under MVC = IRRELEVANT. `site` under PENETRATING = CATEGORICAL qualifier. Same field, three roles, zero code. A bare 'stabbed' qualifies nothing — the site scopes it, exactly as the mechanism scopes a bare '20 feet'.

nodes.prompt: Penetrating injury captured — site not documented. Head, neck, torso, or proximal extremity?
nodes.prompt_with_weapon: {weapon} confirmed — site not captured. Head, neck, torso, or proximal extremity?
nodes._cross_axis: The MECHANISM (gsw/stab) enters the family, but the criterion it satisfies is ANATOMIC — ACS files penetrating injury under anatomic, not mechanism. Cross-axis links are legal and already used: PHYS_AIRWAY derives from an obligation rather than a value. Resolves the ANAT_PENETRATING stub.
nodes._note: affirm/negate empty ON PURPOSE — resolved by the site MODIFIER, exactly as FALL_HEIGHT is resolved by distance. 'GSW to the forearm' is a RESOLVED NEGATIVE (not_applicable), not a gap: the criterion is genuinely not met and the node must never re-ask. Same as a <=20 ft fall.

families.label: Auto vs. pedestrian / bicycle
families.🔴_coverage_warning: DO NOT ADD THIS FAMILY TO THE COVERAGE ARITHMETIC — IT DOUBLE-COUNTS. In NTDB's E-code framework (CDC/MMWR 46:1-30), 'Motor Vehicle Traffic' (25.97%) is an UMBRELLA that already contains Occupant, Motorcyclist, Pedal cyclist AND Pedestrian. Auto-vs-ped is already inside MECH_MVC's 25.97%. This remains a valid LATTICE family — it just cannot enter the sum. (The NTDB 'Pedestrian, other' row at 0.33% is NON-TRAFFIC and is not auto-vs-ped.)
families._stub: ROADMAP ROW — proves the taxonomy extends by DATA. Not exercised by the demo case. Left node-less deliberately; filling it in is a JSON edit, not a code change.

distance.note: A thrown distance. Same rule as MVC.

speed.note: ACS: thrown, run over, or > 20 mph.

families.label: Anatomic criteria (flat lexicon)
families._shape_note: Anatomic criteria are MOSTLY flat presence checks — no walk, no subcategory. Listed here so all three axes live in one file and the walker has one input shape. Two anatomic criteria are genuinely lattice-shaped and are stubbed below.

nodes._note: Satisfied in v2 at T+7:45 by the pelvic binder — a captured-but-NOT-charged event that still feeds fee qualification. Good demo of capture != bill.

negate is load-bearing: v2 T+8:30 says 'sensation intact in both legs'. Also collides with the RSI paralytic — 'paralyzed' at T+3:15 means rocuronium, NOT spinal cord injury. See collision_corpus.jsonl COL-016.

nodes._resolved: STUB RESOLVED 7/17 — was 'ROADMAP: needs a SITE axis'. Now lives in the MECH_PENETRATING family, whose `site` modifier is exactly that axis. Evaluate via PEN_SITE; do not duplicate the logic here. Confirms the roadmap note: it WAS one extra axis and it WAS the same walker — 8.34% of registry coverage for zero code.

nodes._stub: ROADMAP: needs COUNT x PROXIMAL axes. v2 has ONE (open femur) -> does not qualify. Modeling it as flat lexicon would fire on the single femur and fabricate a criterion — which is exactly the failure mode this whole file exists to prevent.

families.label: Physiologic criteria (value predicates)
families._shape_note: PHYSIOLOGIC CRITERIA ARE NEVER PROMPTED AND NEVER WALKED. They are pure predicates over the values-heard stream — you read a BP, you do not ask 'is the systolic under 90?'. They auto-satisfy the instant a qualifying value is heard. The ONLY prompt this axis can produce is the vitals-freshness watch (a STALE value), which is an obligation on the value, not on the criterion. Included here so the three axes share one file; the walker skips shape=predicate entirely.

nodes._note: SATISFIED AT [0:05] BY THE EMS HANDOFF (88/54) — seven seconds before the ejection prompt lands at [0:12]. This single row is the materiality trap in OBLIGATIONS_MODEL.md section 3. Whether it may qualify at all is ADDENDUM Q1: prehospital vs ED-measured.

Tri-state input: total ('GCS thirteen') | components ('eyes to pain, incomprehensible, withdraws') | not_applicable ('not assessable — sedated and paralyzed', T+3:15). The third is NOT missing. See OBLIGATIONS_MODEL.md section 2.
Derived from another obligation, not from a value. Cross-references are legal and this is the demo's example.
ADDENDUM Q2 — MTP starts at T+1:05. Does that count as 'on arrival'? The criterion is timing-scoped and the window is undefined. NOT GUESSED.

orphan_modifier_policy._comment: The 'bare distance, no mechanism captured' case from the RECORDING_PACKET BUILD NOTE. NOTE: this is NOT the demo's case — the demo hears 'high-speed MVC' in the activation call at [0:00], so MECH_MVC IS entered and the distance is an irrelevant modifier under it. The remaining unknown is ejection. That is exactly why BUILD_SPEC's corrected prompt is 'Was the patient ejected?' and not 'fall or ejection?'. This policy covers the harder case where the mechanism never lands at all.

distance.when: a distance is heard and NO mechanism family is entered
distance.prompt: Heard "{distance}" — mechanism not captured. Fall, MVC, or something else?
never: Do not infer a family from the number.
Do not let the number alone satisfy any criterion.
Do not default to fall because 20 ft is a fall threshold — the threshold is a property of the FALL family, not of the number.

rule_table._comment: The RECORDING_PACKET BUILD NOTE table, restated as the assertions this file must satisfy. These are the acceptance tests for the walker.

cases._note: A resolved NEGATIVE, not a gap. Do not re-ask.
THE DEMO CASE. Prompt: 'Was the patient ejected?' — NOT 'fall or ejection?'
site qualifies. weapon=firearm recorded for the registry, contributes nothing to qualification.
A RESOLVED NEGATIVE, not a gap. Distal to the elbow. Do not re-ask. Same shape as a <=20 ft fall.
Prompt: 'Head, neck, torso, or proximal extremity?' A bare 'shot' qualifies nothing — the SITE scopes it, exactly as the mechanism scopes a bare '20 feet'.
enter_guard. A survey ruling penetrating trauma OUT must not enter the family.
Penetrating and blunt genuinely co-occur (shot, then crashed). MECH_PENETRATING has no excludes_families — unlike MVC/fall, these are not mutually exclusive.
